title: कार्तिकेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कार्तिकेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली ॥

ॐ स्कन्दाय नमः।
ॐ गुहाय नमः।
ॐ षण्मुखाय नमः।
ॐ बालनेत्रसुताय नमः।
ॐ प्रभवे नमः।
ॐ पिङ्गलाय नमः।
ॐ कृत्तिकासुनवे नमः।
ॐ शिखिवाहनाय नमः।
ॐ द्विषड्भुजाय नमः।
ॐ द्विषण्णेत्राय नमः॥ १०॥

ॐ शक्तिधराय नमः।
ॐ पिशिताशप्रभञ्जनाय नमः।
ॐ तारकासुरसंहर्त्रे नमः।
ॐ रक्षोबलविमर्दनाय नमः।
ॐ मत्ताय नमः।
ॐ प्रमत्ताय नमः।
ॐ उन्मत्ताय नमः।
ॐ सुरसैन्यसुरक्षकाय नमः।
ॐ देवसेनापतये नमः।
ॐ प्राज्ञाय नमः॥ २०॥

ॐ कृपालवे नमः।
ॐ भक्तवत्सलाय नमः।
ॐ उमासुताय नमः।
ॐ शक्तिधराय नमः।
ॐ कुमाराय नमः।
ॐ क्रौञ्चदारणाय नमः।
ॐ सेनान्ये नमः।
ॐ अग्निजन्मने नमः।
ॐ विशाखाय नमः।
ॐ शङ्करात्मजाय नमः॥ ३०॥

ॐ शिवस्वामिने नमः।
ॐ गणस्वामिने नमः।
ॐ सर्वस्वामिने नमः।
ॐ सनातनाय नमः।
ॐ अनन्तशक्तये नमः।
ॐ अक्षोभ्याय नमः।
ॐ पार्वतीप्रियनन्दनाय नमः।
ॐ गङ्गासुताय नमः।
ॐ शरोद्भवाय नमः।
ॐ आहूताय नमः॥ ४०॥

ॐ पावकात्मजाय नमः।
ॐ जृम्भाय नमः।
ॐ प्रजृम्भाय नमः।
ॐ उज्जृम्भाय नमः।
ॐ कमलासनसंस्तुताय नमः।
ॐ एकवर्णाय नमः।
ॐ द्विवर्णाय नमः।
ॐ त्रिवर्णाय नमः।
ॐ सुमनोहराय नमः।
ॐ चतुर्वर्णाय नमः॥ ५०॥

ॐ पञ्चवर्णाय नमः।
ॐ प्रजापतये नमः।
ॐ अहर्पतये नमः।
ॐ अग्निगर्भाय नमः।
ॐ शमीगर्भाय नमः।
ॐ विश्वरेतसे नमः।
ॐ सुरारिघ्ने नमः।
ॐ हरिद्वर्णाय नमः।
ॐ शुभकराय नमः।
ॐ वटवे नमः॥ ६०॥

ॐ वटुवेषभृते नमः।
ॐ पूषणे नमः।
ॐ गभस्तये नमः।
ॐ गहनाय नमः।
ॐ चन्द्रवर्णाय नमः।
ॐ कालनाशनाय नमः।
ॐ कन्दर्पाय नमः।
ॐ कामदाय नमः।
ॐ कान्ताय नमः।
ॐ वेलाय नमः॥ ७०॥

ॐ महाबलाय नमः।
ॐ सिद्धार्थाय नमः।
ॐ सिद्धिदाय नमः।
ॐ सिद्धाय नमः।
ॐ सिद्धसन्कल्पाय नमः।
ॐ अमोघाय नमः।
ॐ अरिष्टनेमिने नमः।
ॐ धर्माध्यक्षाय नमः।
ॐ महानराय नमः।
ॐ पुरुषस्तुत्याय नमः॥ ८०॥

ॐ आत्मभुवे नमः।
ॐ वेदगर्भाय नमः।
ॐ विरात्स्वराजे नमः।
ॐ वीरवाहाय नमः।
ॐ शूरसेनाय नमः।
ॐ विधानजननाय नमः।
ॐ वत्सलाय नमः।
ॐ अमृतवाचे नमः।
ॐ अमृतवपुषे नमः।
ॐ यज्ञभुजे नमः॥ ९०॥

ॐ शत्रुसूदनाय नमः।
ॐ विश्वकर्मणे नमः।
ॐ विश्वदेवाय नमः।
ॐ अनेकशस्त्रविशारदाय नमः।
ॐ सुब्रह्मण्याय नमः।
ॐ गुहप्रियाय नमः।
ॐ ज्ञानगम्याय नमः।
ॐ दृढव्रताय नमः।
ॐ कृष्णमायाविमुक्ताय नमः।
ॐ वल्लीदेवसेनापतये नमः॥ १००॥

ॐ शूरपद्मासुरहराय नमः।
ॐ षडाक्षरमन्त्ररूपाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वरोगप्रशमनाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वपापप्रणाशनाय नमः।
ॐ तत्त्वज्ञानप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ मयूरवाहनाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वसिद्धिप्रदाय नमः।
ॐ श्रीसुब्रह्मण्यपरब्रह्मणे नमः॥ १०८॥

॥ इति श्री कार्तिकेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali ||

Om Skandaya Namah.
Om Guhaya Namah.
Om Shanmukhaya Namah.
Om Balanetra-sutaya Namah.
Om Prabhave Namah.
Om Pingalaya Namah.
Om Krittika-sunave Namah.
Om Shikhi-vahanaya Namah.
Om Dvishadbhujaya Namah.
Om Dvishannetraya Namah. || 10 ||

Om Shakti-dharaya Namah.
Om Pishitasha-prabhanjnaya Namah.
Om Tarakasura-samhartre Namah.
Om Rakshobala-vimardanaya Namah.
Om Mattaya Namah.
Om Pramattaya Namah.
Om Unmattaya Namah.
Om Sura-sainya-surakshakaya Namah.
Om Devasenapataye Namah.
Om Prajnaya Namah. || 20 ||

Om Kripalave Namah.
Om Bhaktavatsalaya Namah.
Om Uma-sutaya Namah.
Om Shaktidharaya Namah.
Om Kumaraya Namah.
Om Krauncha-daranaya Namah.
Om Senanye Namah.
Om Agnijanmane Namah.
Om Vishakhaya Namah.
Om Shankaratmajaya Namah. || 30 ||

Om Shivasvamine Namah.
Om Ganasvamine Namah.
Om Sarvasvamine Namah.
Om Sanatanaya Namah.
Om Anantashaktaye Namah.
Om Akshobhyaya Namah.
Om Parvati-priya-nandanaya Namah.
Om Gangasutaya Namah.
Om Sharodbhavaya Namah.
Om Ahutaya Namah. || 40 ||

Om Pavakatmajaya Namah.
Om Jrimbhaya Namah.
Om Prajrimbhaya Namah.
Om Ujjrimbhaya Namah.
Om Kamalasana-samstutaya Namah.
Om Ekavarnaya Namah.
Om Dvivarnaya Namah.
Om Trivarnaya Namah.
Om Sumanoharaya Namah.
Om Chaturvarnaya Namah. || 50 ||

Om Panchavarnaya Namah.
Om Prajapataye Namah.
Om Aharpataye Namah.
Om Agnigarbhaya Namah.
Om Shamigarbhaya Namah.
Om Vishvaretase Namah.
Om Surarigne Namah.
Om Haridvarnaya Namah.
Om Shubhakaraya Namah.
Om Vatave Namah. || 60 ||

Om Vatuvesha-bhrite Namah.
Om Pushane Namah.
Om Gabhastaye Namah.
Om Gahanaya Namah.
Om Chandravarnaya Namah.
Om Kalanashanaya Namah.
Om Kandarpaya Namah.
Om Kamadaya Namah.
Om Kantaya Namah.
Om Velaya Namah. || 70 ||

Om Mahabalaya Namah.
Om Siddharthaya Namah.
Om Siddhidaya Namah.
Om Siddhaya Namah.
Om Siddha-sankalpaya Namah.
Om Amoghaya Namah.
Om Arishtanemine Namah.
Om Dharmadhyakshaya Namah.
Om Mahanaraya Namah.
Om Purusha-stutyaya Namah. || 80 ||

Om Atmabhuve Namah.
Om Vedagarbhaya Namah.
Om Virat-svaraje Namah.
Om Viravahaya Namah.
Om Shurasenaya Namah.
Om Vidhana-jananaya Namah.
Om Vatsalaya Namah.
Om Amrita-vache Namah.
Om Amrita-vapushe Namah.
Om Yajnabhuje Namah. || 90 ||

Om Shatru-sudanaya Namah.
Om Vishvakarmane Namah.
Om Vishvadevaya Namah.
Om Aneka-shastra-visharadaya Namah.
Om Subrahmanyaya Namah.
Om Guhapriyaya Namah.
Om Jnanagamyaya Namah.
Om Dridhavrataya Namah.
Om Krishna-maya-vimuktaya Namah.
Om Valli-Devasenapataye Namah. || 100 ||

Om Shurapadma-asura-haraya Namah.
Om Shadakshara-mantra-rupaya Namah.
Om Sarvaroga-prashamanaya Namah.
Om Sarvapapa-pranashanaya Namah.
Om Tattvajnana-pradaya Namah.
Om Mayura-vahanaya Namah.
Om Sarvasiddhi-pradaya Namah.
Om Shri-Subrahmanya-Parabrahmane Namah. || 108 ||

|| Iti Shri Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali Sampoorna ||
hindiMeaning: कार्तिकेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली में भगवान कार्तिकेय (स्कन्द, षण्मुख, सुब्रह्मण्य, गुह, कुमार) के 108 दिव्य नामों का संग्रह है। वे शिव-पार्वती के पुत्र, कृत्तिकाओं द्वारा पालित, मयूरवाहन, शक्तिधर, देवसेनापति, तारकासुरसंहारी और क्रौञ्चदारण हैं। पूजा-अर्चना में पुष्पार्पण के लिए यह नामावली विशेष उपयोगी है।
benefits: Perfect for archana worship of Lord Kartikeya with 108 names
Invokes victory over enemies and obstacles
Invokes the blessing of courage, wisdom, and divine knowledge
Traditionally recited for removal of diseases and sins
Especially powerful on Skanda Shashthi and Tuesdays
description: The Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali, known in Sanskrit as कार्तिकेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली, is a devotional recitation of 108 sacred names in praise of Lord Kartikeya. Attributed to Skanda Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove diseases and sins as well as grant victory over enemies and obstacles. Additionally, it is known to bestow courage, wisdom, and divine knowledge. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Skanda Shashti. Comprising 108 verses, it can be completed in approximately 12 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali - 108 names of Lord Kartikeya for archana in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning.
relatedSearches: Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali lyrics Sanskrit PDF
108 names of Kartikeya Murugan Subrahmanya meaning
Kartikeya namavali for victory and wisdom
Lord Murugan 108 names with meaning Sanskrit Hindi

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali (कार्तिकेय अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली) is a sacred enumeration of 108 names of Lord Kartikeya — known variously as Skanda, Subrahmanya, Shanmukha, Guha, Kumara, Murugan and Devasenapati — preserved in the Skanda Purana and recited as part of formal archana worship. The namavali format (each name prefixed with Om and suffixed with Namah) is specifically designed for kumkum, akshata or pushpa archana, where one name is offered with each flower or grain of rice. The 108 names cover his birth from the six Krittikas, his role as Devasenapati who slew Tarakasura and Surapadma, his identity as son of Shiva-Parvati and brother of Ganesha, and his final form as Subrahmanya Parabrahman. (Source: Skanda Purana; classical Kartikeya stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the most important names of Kartikeya in this Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The 108 names traverse the entire Kartikeya theology. Origin names: Skanda, Guha, Shanmukha (six-faced), Krittikasunu (son of the Krittikas), Agnijanma (born of Agni), Sharodbhava (born in the sara grass), Pavakatmaja, Gangasuta. Iconic names: Shaktidhara (wielder of the Shakti spear), Shikhivahana / Mayuravahana (peacock-mounted), Dvishadbhuja (twelve-armed), Dvishannetra (twelve-eyed). Heroic names: Tarakasura-samhartri, Krauncha-darana (cleaver of Mt Krauncha), Shurapadma-asura-hara, Devasenapati. Divine identity names: Shankaratmaja, Uma-suta, Parvati-priya-nandana, Subrahmanya, Shri-Subrahmanya-Parabrahman, Shadakshara-mantra-rupa. The list culminates by declaring Kartikeya as Para Brahman itself. (Source: 108-name list within the stotra.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali used in archana worship?
seoFaqs.answer: In traditional Kartikeya / Murugan temples and home shrines, the Ashtottara Shatanamavali is recited during pushpa-archana, kumkum-archana or akshata-archana — one name is uttered with each flower, pinch of vermilion or grain of rice offered at the feet of the deity. The format (Om + name in dative + Namah, e.g. Om Skandaya Namah, Om Mayura-vahanaya Namah) is specifically designed for this purpose. After completing all 108 names, the devotee performs neerajana (arati) and prarthana. In the Tamil Murugan tradition, this archana is daily ritual at the six Padaiveedu temples and in countless household pujas. (Source: Murugan / Subrahmanya temple agama; classical archana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Kartikeya 108 namavali be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali is most powerful on Tuesday (Mangalvar — for victory) and on Shashthi tithi of every month (Subrahmanya's tithi), and is especially recited during Skanda Shashthi (Aippasi / Kartik Shukla Shashthi — when Kartikeya defeated Surapadma), Thaipusam (Magha full moon — celebrated at Palani and Batu Caves), Vaikasi Visakham (Vaishakha Vishakha day — Kartikeya's birthday), Panguni Uttiram (Phalguni Uttara — wedding to Devasena and Valli), and during Soorasamharam. The namavali is also chanted during Mandala vrata (41 days), in Murugan temples on every Krittika nakshatra day, and as nityapaath by Subrahmanya devotees seeking victory, wisdom or removal of disease. (Source: Skanda Shashthi paddhati; Tamil Murugan tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional teachers attribute the following benefits to regular archana with the Kartikeya 108 names: victory over enemies and obstacles (Kartikeya as Devasenapati and Tarakasura-samhartri); courage and strength (as Mahabala, Shurasena, Mahaviryah); wisdom and divine knowledge (as Jnanagamya, Vedagarbha, Tattvajnana-prada — Kartikeya as the Pranava-acharya who taught Om to Shiva himself); removal of all diseases (Sarvaroga-prashamana); destruction of all sins (Sarvapapa-pranashana); fulfilment of all spiritual siddhis (Sarvasiddhi-prada); protection of children (as Bala-pala); blessing for marriage (through his Valli-Devasena connection); and ultimately moksha through identification with Subrahmanya as Parabrahman. (Sources: namavali phalashruti; Skanda Purana; Subrahmanya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali differ from the Kartikeya Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kartikeya Ashtottara Shatanamavali is a 108-name list optimized for daily archana — short enough to complete in 12-15 minutes and ideal for routine pushpa-arpana. The Kartikeya / Subrahmanya Sahasranama, by contrast, is a 1000-name compendium (preserved in the Skanda Purana and in the Rudra Yamala) that is recited on major Shashthi celebrations, special vrata days and during longer parayanas. The Sahasranama covers far more rare epithets and Tantric details, while the Ashtottara Shatanamavali contains the core 108 most beloved and theologically central names. Devotees typically recite the Ashtottara daily and the Sahasranama on Skanda Shashthi, Thaipusam and other major Murugan festivals. (Source: classical Kartikeya stotra corpus.)
